zotero.abstractNote: Bayesian neural networks (BNNs) are often praised for their ability to quantify uncertainty. However, it has recently been shown that standard BNNs with ReLU activations are asymptotically overconfident: as the input moves away from the training data, the predictive distribution collapses to a point mass. We show that this behavior is a consequence of the finite-width architecture and the specific form of the ReLU activation. To fix this, we propose an infinite-feature extension for Bayesian ReLU nets. We show that this extension is equivalent to a Gaussian process with a non-stationary kernel that does not suffer from the asymptotic overconfidence problem. We provide a practical, scalable implementation and demonstrate that our method yields well-calibrated uncertainty estimates on out-of-distribution data while maintaining competitive predictive performance.
zotero.conferenceName: Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems 34 (NeurIPS 2021)
zotero.proceedingsTitle: Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems 34 (NeurIPS 2021)
